MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-24-2016 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And/Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE COMPANY and FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE COMPANY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And/Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication is GRANTED. No opposition was filed. Prevailing party to submit a [proposed] written order tracking, verbatim the Court's ruling and in compliance with C.C.P. Section 437c(g).

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #59761, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

Off Calendar Mar-16-2016 Set For Trial On Nov-07-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-04-2016 Continued To Mar-30-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-08-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Terrence Mayo of Mayo & Mayo telephone number 415-397-1515 for Plaintiff.
Matthew Angell of Angell Law Firm telephone number 925-947-2525 for Defendant.

The matter was discussed/argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court ruled as follows: DEFENDANT ADRIENNE BLALOCK'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED VERIFIED COMPLAINT is overruled as to all causes of action except the 2nd and 3rd causes of action where the demurrer is sustained with 20-days leave to amend to give the plaintiff opportunities to allege frauds with greater specificity. The prevailing party is to prepare a form of order and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/HK)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-21-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RHETT JOHNSON OF THE FIDELITY NATIONAL LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 1-925-260-2265 EXT. 65 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE COMPANY and FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE COMPANY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION TO STRIKE claims for attorney's fees and for a constructive trust against the fidelity defendants from the 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted without leave to amend. Pursuant to CCP 435 and 436 the court will grant the Fidelity Defendants' motion to strike the eleventh and twelfth causes of actions in the First Amended Complaint. The court will strike the cause of action for attorney fees because Plaintiff has failed to allege facts which establish the existence of an agreement entitling her to attorney fees, Plaintiff has failed to cite a statute under which she may bring her action for attorney fees, and the tort of another doctrine does not apply in this case. The court will strike the twelfth cause of action for a constructive trust because Plaintiff has merely made a request for a remedy without pleading the elements which would establish entitlement to a constructive trust. Furthermore, Plaintiff admits in 47 of the First Amended Complaint that the Fidelity Defendants are no longer in possession of a res upon which a constructive trust may be imposed. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-27-2015 Continued To Jan-20-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
